ddls:
CREATE TABLE "policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who purchased the policy */,
  "policy_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy type (e.g., health, auto, home) */,
  "premium_id" INTEGER /* ID of the premium (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "sales_channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sales channel through which the policy was sold */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region where the policy was sold */,
  "vehicle_age_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vehicle age (e.g., < 1 Year, 1-2 Year, > 2 Years) */,
  "vehicle_damage_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vehicle damage (e.g., Yes, No) */,
  "vintage" INTEGER /* Vintage of the policy (i.e., the number of days the policy has been active) */,
  "policy_status" TEXT /* Status of the policy (e.g., active, inactive, cancelled) */,
  "policy_date" TEXT /* Date when the policy was purchased */,
  "policy_description" TEXT /* Description of the policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_sales_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("sales_channel_id") REFERENCES sales_channels ("sales_channel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_vehicle_age_id FOREIGN KEY ("vehicle_age_id") REFERENCES vehicle_ages ("vehicle_age_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_vehicle_damage_id FOREIGN KEY ("vehicle_damage_id") REFERENCES vehicle_damages ("vehicle_damage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_policy_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_type_id") REFERENCES policy_types ("policy_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_premium_id FOREIGN KEY ("premium_id") REFERENCES premiums ("premium_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "age" REAL /* Age of the customer */,
  "driving_license" INTEGER /* Driving license status of the customer (e.g., 0, 1) */,
  "previously_insured" INTEGER /* Whether the customer was previously insured (e.g., 0, 1) */,
  "annual_income" REAL /* Annual income of the customer */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the customer */,
  "education" TEXT /* Education level of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "region_code" INTEGER /* Code of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_channels" (
  "sales_channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales channel */,
  "sales_channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the sales channel */,
  "sales_channel_description" TEXT /* Description of the sales channel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sales_channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_types" (
  "policy_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy type */,
  "policy_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the policy type */,
  "policy_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the policy type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "premiums" (
  "premium_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each premium */,
  "premium_name" TEXT /* Name of the premium (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "premium_amount" REAL /* Amount of the premium */,
  "premium_description" TEXT /* Description of the premium */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("premium_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vehicle_ages" (
  "vehicle_age_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vehicle age */,
  "vehicle_age_name" TEXT /* Name of the vehicle age (e.g., < 1 Year, 1-2 Year, > 2 Years) */,
  "vehicle_age_description" TEXT /* Description of the vehicle age */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vehicle_age_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vehicle_damages" (
  "vehicle_damage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vehicle damage */,
  "vehicle_damage_name" TEXT /* Name of the vehicle damage (e.g., Yes, No) */,
  "vehicle_damage_description" TEXT /* Description of the vehicle damage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vehicle_damage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy being responded to */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer responding */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date when the response was made */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response (e.g., accepted, rejected) */,
  "response_description" TEXT /* Description of the response */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., sales agent, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics record */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy being analyzed */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer being analyzed */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response (e.g., accepted, rejected) */,
  "sale_date" TEXT /* Date when the sale was made */,
  "sale_amount" REAL /* Amount of the sale */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the policy was sold */,
  "sales_channel" TEXT /* Sales channel through which the policy was sold */,
  "policy_type" TEXT /* Type of policy sold */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "claims" (
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each claim */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy being claimed */,
  "claim_date" TEXT /* Date when the claim was made */,
  "claim_amount" REAL /* Amount of the claim */,
  "claim_description" TEXT /* Description of the claim */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("claim_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_claims_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "insured" (
  "insured_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each insured record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer being tracked */,
  "previously_insured" INTEGER /* Whether the customer was previously insured (e.g., 0, 1) */,
  "insured_amount" REAL /* Amount of insurance coverage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("insured_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_insured_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "licenses" (
  "license_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each license record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer being tracked */,
  "driving_license" INTEGER /* Driving license status of the customer (e.g., 0, 1) */,
  "license_issue_date" TEXT /* Date when the license was issued */,
  "license_expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the license expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("license_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_licenses_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_activity" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the activity */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date when the activity was performed */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., login, logout, policy sale) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_activity_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who received the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., policy renewal, claim approved) */,
  "notification_message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
